senses.definition: Relating to or produced by heat; having a high caloric value.
examples: Calorific value measures how much energy is released when fuel is burned.
A calorific diet is one that provides many calories.

senses.definition: Relating to calorific properties; of or pertaining to heat energy.
examples: The calorifical analysis showed how the substance released energy.
Calorifical measurements were recorded in the study.

senses.definition: In a calorific manner; in terms of heat energy or calories.
examples: The food was calorifically rich, providing a lot of energy.
Calorifically, the experiment released a large amount of heat.

senses.definition: The process of adding heat or the conversion of a substance to a calorific form; the production of heat energy.
examples: Calorification occurred as the reaction released energy.
The calorification of the fuel was measured.

senses.definition: Plural of calorification; instances of heat production or calorific processes.
examples: We studied several calorifications in the experiment.
Different foods have varying calorifications.

senses.definition: Calorific values; units or measures of energy content in foods or fuels.
examples: The calorifics of the meal were calculated.
Energy labels show calorifics per serving.

senses.definition: A device or element that converts energy into heat; in plumbing, a vessel that heats water.
examples: The boiler includes a calorifier for hot water storage.
Replace the calorifier to improve efficiency.

senses.definition: Multiple devices or vessels that heat water or generate heat energy.
examples: The factory installed new calorifiers.
Calorifiers vary in capacity.

senses.definition: To convert something into heat energy or to increase its calorific value.
examples: The fuel is used to calorify the system.
Researchers aim to calorify the compound for energy release.

senses.definition: Causing or promoting the production of heat or heat-related energy.
examples: Certain reactions are calorigenic and release heat.
Glucose oxidation is calorigenic.

senses.definition: An instrument used to measure the amount of heat involved in a chemical reaction or other process.
examples: A calorimeter was used to measure the energy released.
Scientists calculated calorimeter readings to determine calorific values.

senses.definition: Multiple instruments that measure heat in reactions or materials.
examples: Different laboratories use various calorimeters.
Calorimeters help compare energy release.

senses.definition: Related to calorimetry; describing measurements of heat changes in chemical reactions.
examples: Calorimetric data supported the hypothesis.
The calorimetric method was used in the study.

senses.definition: Relating to calorimetry; alternative form of calorimetric.
examples: Calorimetrical data indicated heat release.
The calorimetrical method yielded precise results.

senses.definition: In a calorimetric manner; by calorimetry.
examples: The sample was analyzed calorimetrically.
Results were established calorimetrically.

senses.definition: Plural of calorimetry; the practice or science of measuring heat changes.
examples: The lab compares several calorimetries.
Advances in calorimetries improve energy testing.

senses.definition: The science of measuring heat changes in chemical reactions or physical processes.
examples: Calorimetry helps determine energy content.
We performed calorimetry to study the reaction.

senses.definition: A device or mechanism that converts heat into motion; a hypothetical or specialized term not widely used.
examples: The old machine included a calorimotor component.
Calorimotor principles were discussed in the lecture.

senses.definition: Plural of caloris; a Latin term sometimes used in science to mean heat or warmth, but rare in modern English.
examples: Ancient texts mention caloris in relation to warmth.
In some glossaries, caloris appears as a root meaning heat.

senses.definition: A device that generates or modulates heat; a rarely used term.
examples: The calorisator heated the sample.
A calorisator was replaced in the apparatus.

senses.definition: British spelling of calorize: to heat or add heat to something; to make warmer.
examples: They plan to calorise the solution.
The sun will calorise the metal object.

senses.definition: Past tense of calorise; heated or warmed.
examples: The metal was calorised before testing.
The food was calorised briefly.

senses.definition: Third person singular of calorise; heats or warms something.
examples: The heater calorises the room slowly.
It calorises the oil to the right temperature.

senses.definition: British spelling of calorizing; heating or warming, especially in a process.
examples: They are calorising the sample to observe changes.
Calorising is part of the preparation.

senses.definition: A person who studies or works with calorimetry; an expert in measuring heat changes.
examples: The calorist presented data at the conference.
As a calorist, she specializes in energy measurements.
